description: A weathered man with tired eyes sits heavily, the weight of leadership evident in his posture.

introduction: *An older man with silver hair looks up from his untouched meal*
You're new in town. I can always tell.
*He gestures wearily to an empty chair*
Sit, if you've got a moment. I'm Mayor Aldwin.
These days, I spend more time here than at the town hall.
Hard to focus on ledgers when people are... disappearing.

options.text: Perhaps I can help with these disappearances.
response: *His eyes sharpen with sudden interest*
Help? Most folk want to pack up and leave when they hear the truth.
*He leans forward intently*
But if you're serious... we need it desperately.
Three families gone in the past month. No trace, no ransom demands.
Started with travelers and drifters. Now it's people with roots.
*His voice drops to a whisper*
My own wife and daughter... taken two weeks ago.
Whatever's doing this is organized, methodical. It's not random.

options.text: Tell me about Redstone. It seems troubled.
response: *He sighs deeply*
Redstone was a prosperous place once. Trade routes, good harvests.
But something's been hunting our people for months now.
*He stares into his ale*
We've sent word to the capital, but help is weeks away.
Meanwhile, families lock their doors at sunset.
The town watch does what they can, but they're scared too.

options.text: You carry a heavy burden for this town.
response: *He nods grimly*
The weight of leadership becomes heavier when you're failing.
Every morning I wake hoping for good news, and every evening...
*He trails off, then looks directly at you*
But dwelling on it won't bring them back.
Action will. The question is what kind of action.
Are you someone who acts, or someone who just offers sympathy?

introduction: *The Mayor looks up with weary hope as you approach*
Have you made any progress on the investigation?
Please, tell me you've found something. Anything.
Every day that passes, more people live in fear.
And I wonder if my family is still...

options.text: Where should I focus my investigation?
response: *He pulls out a worn map and spreads it on the table*
Three places keep coming up in the reports.
*He points to different areas*
The old Swamp Church - abandoned for years, but lights seen at night.
Hill Ruins outside town - local hunters avoid the area now.
And there's a refugee camp beyond the forest.
*He looks up seriously*
All three have connections to the disappearances.
But be warned - others have investigated and not returned.

options.text: I'll need trustworthy companions for this.
response: *He nods emphatically*
Absolutely. This isn't work for someone alone.
*He gestures toward the tavern patrons*
There are good people here who've been waiting for someone to lead.
Gareth, the warrior - he's been itching to act.
Elara knows magic, Thorman's got the divine blessing.
And Lyra... well, she has skills others don't.
*He leans closer*
They respect action over words. Show them you're serious,
and they'll follow you into whatever danger awaits.
*Relief crosses his features*
Good. That's the smart approach.
Too many have rushed in without preparation.
*He clasps your shoulder*
Take the time you need, but please don't take too long.
Every night, families wonder if they'll see the next dawn.
When you're ready to move, the town will support you.

introduction: *The Mayor's face lights up as he sees you*
Please, even small discoveries give people hope.
The town needs to know someone capable is working on this.

response: *His expression becomes grave*
Tell me everything. No matter how dark.
*He steels himself*
I need to know what we're truly facing.
The people deserve to understand the danger,
even if the truth is harder than uncertainty.

options.text: Still gathering information, but making progress.
response: *He nods understandingly*
Thorough work takes time. I appreciate your diligence.
*He looks around the tavern*
Just knowing someone competent is on this helps morale.
Take what time you need, but stay safe out there.

accept_investigation.text: I accept the responsibility of investigating.
accept_investigation.description: Formally agree to help solve the disappearances
response: *He stands and grasps your hand firmly*
Thank you. You may be our only hope.
I hereby deputize you!
You have my full authority to go where needed and ask what questions you must.
*He reaches into his coat*
This badge will identify you to any town guard or official.
May the gods watch over you in this dark business.

ask_details.text: Tell me exactly what happened to the missing people.
ask_details.description: Get specific information about the disappearances
response: It always happens the same way. People go missing at night.
No signs of struggle, no broken doors or windows.
*His hands clench*
My investigation found traces leading toward three locations:
An old church in the swamps, ancient ruins on the hill,
and reports of strange activity near the refugee camp.

express_condolences.description: Offer sympathy for his personal tragedy
response: *He nods solemnly*
Thank you. That means more than you know.
*He straightens with renewed determination*
But I can't let grief paralyze me. Others need protection.
I have to believe action can still save them.

ask_swamp_details.text: Tell me about this Swamp Church.
response: *He points to the southern area of the map*
Abandoned temple, maybe fifty years now.
Used to serve the outer farms before they were abandoned.
*He frowns*
Hunters report seeing lights there at night.
And some of the missing were last seen heading that direction.
*He indicates the eastern hills*
Ancient stone structures, older than the town.
Always been rumored to be haunted, but recently...
*He lowers his voice*
People hear chanting carried on the wind.
And the local hunters refuse to go near anymore.

ask_refugee_details.text: What's this about a refugee camp?
response: *His expression grows troubled*
Displaced people from the southern provinces.
They set up camp a few months ago, seemed harmless enough.
*He hesitates*
But some of our missing were last seen near their camp.
Could be coincidence, but... we can't ignore any possibility.

metadata.author: Terror in Redstone Development
metadata.notes: Keystone dialogue that activates recruitment system and quest progression. Sets mayor_talked flag and reveals investigation locations.

narrative_schema_compliance.quest_integration: Primary quest catalyst and information hub
